name: Sui's Garden of Grotesqueries
line:
Near Baizao, nestled between mounts and stream, tourists enjoy themselves.
With the garden cleared of bystanders, prepare for battle.
Throw wide the gates, and look down from the peaks. The Placation begins.

zones.name: Honglu Tower
zones.desc: All visitors here to view the Garden are as one family.

zones.name: Shanshui Pavilion
zones.desc: Picturesque sights are for the spirits too. Beware the soil, trees, grass, and stones.

zones.name: Yunwa Gazebo
zones.desc: Look up to see the clouds, look down to see the roofs. The road ahead has many hindrances, its crags hard to traverse.

zones.name: Ruwu Gate
zones.desc: Heaven and earth are vast, mount and river are fierce, but none as great as thou, none as great as I.

zones.name: Jianzi Shrine
zones.desc: Though named Jianzi Shrine, you see not a single character on its plaques. For all that transpires before and within the shrine cannot be recorded.

zones.name: Shimo Tomb
zones.desc: From this comes life. Do not believe it is the end.

zones.name: Mingmie Peak
zones.desc: The scroll appears, and the path ahead opens. Here, beginnings and endings, births and deaths, truths and lies converge.

zones.name: Laiqu Point
zones.desc: Seek both past and present. Within the ever-changing ending, seek your destiny.

nodeTypes.name: Operation
nodeTypes.desc: The nonsensical shall reveal its hidden truths in your hands.
nodeTypes.func: A standard combat node. Win to earn collectibles, Originium Ingots, and other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Emergency Ops
nodeTypes.desc: Life and death hang in the balance. Strive for your very best.
nodeTypes.func: A harder fight with stronger enemies than regular Combat Ops — and better rewards (Hope, etc.) to match.

nodeTypes.name: Dreadful Foe
nodeTypes.desc: Whether you be valiant hero or timid musbeast depends on how you deal with this battle.
nodeTypes.func: The boss battle guarding the end of each floor. You must clear it to move on to the next floor.

nodeTypes.name: Safe House
nodeTypes.desc: Halfway up the mount. Catch your breath at a bench.
nodeTypes.func: A rest node offering upkeep options such as restoring Objective HP or temporarily promoting Operators.

nodeTypes.name: Encounter
nodeTypes.desc: Every curiosity under Heaven can be found in the Garden. Fret not, for you shall see and meet them.
nodeTypes.func: A random-event node. Depending on your choices you gain rewards — or pay a price.

nodeTypes.name: Downtime Recreation
nodeTypes.desc: Indulging in life's simpler pleasures helps combat the monotony and hardships of your long journey ahead.
nodeTypes.func: A recreation node with theme-specific minigames and wagers. Rewards depend on the outcome.

nodeTypes.name: Dense Fog
nodeTypes.desc: There's something in the mist, but you cannot see anything...
nodeTypes.func: An unidentified node hidden from view. Its true type is revealed when you enter.

nodeTypes.name: Wish Fulfilled
nodeTypes.desc: While the air may not be suffused with divine power, a sincere wish will always come true.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you pick one recruitment voucher from a random selection.

nodeTypes.name: Lost and Found
nodeTypes.desc: What things are worth, I shall weigh.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you trade what you have (Objective HP, collectibles, etc.) for other rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Scout
nodeTypes.desc: Set off on your own to see what you will.
nodeTypes.func: A node that sends some of your Operators on a detached mission. They leave your squad for a while and bring back rewards on return.

nodeTypes.name: Rogue Trader
nodeTypes.desc: He may be a foreigner, but he understands Yanese etiquette well enough.
nodeTypes.func: A shop where you spend Originium Ingots on collectibles and items, or restore Objective HP.

nodeTypes.name: Wander into Wonderland
nodeTypes.desc: Take up the name of Sui, and step into the border of truth and lies.
nodeTypes.func: An entrance to the special area beyond the Garden.

nodeTypes.name: False to Truth
nodeTypes.desc: The changes are so remarkably real that you think you can tell true from false.
nodeTypes.func: A refining node that converts materials you put in into something else.

nodeTypes.name: Face-Off
nodeTypes.desc: When meeting face-to-face, the more capable one wins.
nodeTypes.func: A special battle where you choose your opponent. Losing costs no Objective HP, and harder opponents give better rewards.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc: Think first, then act. There is no turning back.
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

nodeTypes.name: Prophecy
nodeTypes.desc: Think first, then act. There is no turning back.
nodeTypes.func: A story-event node tied to ending branches. It appears at specific spots depending on your progress and holdings, and can lead to hidden ending routes.

nodeTypes.name: Wander into Wonderland
nodeTypes.desc: Take up the name of Sui, and step into the border of truth and lies.
nodeTypes.func: An entrance to the special area beyond the Garden.

nodeTypes.name: Pathfinder
nodeTypes.desc: Clarity of soul shall reveal the light of Heaven.
nodeTypes.func: A node where you can use stashed recruitment vouchers. Using them here reduces the Hope cost.

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Higher starting HP, enemies in Sui's Remnant Consciousness are extremely weak

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Starting Max Life Points -2

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Hour of Sui influence is stronger

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: More Dayan Tongbao in starting coin coffer

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: All enemies have +40% HP, Talisman HP +50%

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: All enemies take 10% less Physical and Arts damage

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Effect increased from Dim to Clear when Hour of Sui changes

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Dayan Tongbao in starting coin coffer changed to special Risk Coins, enemies in Sui's Remnant Consciousness are no longer weakened

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: 5-star Operator Hope cost +1, Elite and leader enemy DEF and HP +20%

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Number of battles in Sui's Remnant Consciousness increased, Deployment Limit -1

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Operators with talismans have reduced ATK, starting Max Life Points -2

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Recruitment Voucher conservation limit reduced, enemy ATK +20%

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Hour of Sui effect increased from Clear to Acute, refreshing shop and retossing coins cost more Coupons

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Max HP +50%; 'Yi' gains more Barrier when Hui-Ming status changes

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Leader enemies take 20% less damage; 'Sui' gains increased ATK; Operators defeated by the slap attack of 'Sui' have greatly increased Redeployment Time

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: 6-star Operator Hope cost +1, Operators with talismans deal damage and Burn to self and surrounding units, 'Xia' gains +50% ATK and HP

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: All enemies have ASPD +20; 'Bestial Hou' has increased HP, 'Bestial Ju' has increased ATK, 'Bestial Hou' summons different enemies

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Leader enemies have ATK +20%; Hope cost reduction for recruiting or promoting Operators with lit candles reduced to 1; 'Drunken Stupor' and 'Flame of Intrigue' effects increase, 'Restrained Pride' and 'Fume of Anger' have increased ATK

difficulties.name: Guided Tour
difficulties.rule: Retossing coins will cost more Coupons; more enemies will appear in some battles; 'Vessel of Shuo' Dodges all Physical and Arts damage from units not blocked by itself

difficulties.name: Sightseers' Scroll
difficulties.rule: Squads are refreshed on the first day of each month

stages.name: Whack-A-Mus
stages.desc: Legend says the musbeasts of the Garden of Grotesqueries love to nibble on ancient tomes of poetry. Visitors who recite sordid poems or vulgar words will hear jeering chitters from the shadows, and chase after them to hidden burrows, ultimately provoking furious battles.

stages.name: To Market
stages.desc: Occasionally, a group of people will line up in squares in an empty space. When asked, they say: 'We're going to market.' With the strike of a gong, they head off on a tottering march, destroying all that blocks the way. A startling sight for onlookers.

stages.name: Serious Business
stages.desc:
You can't defend a business with only your mouth. But it does seem like Big Bob has misunderstood why you've come to his shop.
 are present on the field that Block enemies

stages.name: Veteran Actor
stages.desc: You are cordially invited to watch the play, but the theater is deserted. There is a sudden burst of song from the shoddy stage as if urging you to stay, making the scene stranger than it already is.

stages.name: Pest Control
stages.desc: In the Garden of Grotesqueries, pest control is generally done by the crops themselves. In their minds, if you are not a farmer, then you are a pest.

stages.name: Ticket Please
stages.desc: It seems that the hubbub when the Garden opens has made its mark on the entrance, to the point that when it closes, naive geists are all too eager to imitate the tourists crowding into the gate to get their tickets inspected.

stages.name: Learning for All
stages.desc: At the renovation site, the workers are eagerly listening to a lesson taught by a 'Tianshi'. 'Uneducated men are no different from geists!' As you pass by, you are pulled into the 'classroom' by the strict 'Tianshi'.

stages.name: Look Out Above
stages.desc:
Flying Oxmanes and fleeing Hornbeasts are causing a ruckus in the Garden. Where did the throwing device that started it all come from? A geist tinkerer pats the sawdust off his body in a corner of the garden, leaving in satisfaction.
 Periodically fires a shell forward, dealing Physical splash damage; can launch a nearby ground enemy of 3 Weight or less to the center of the explosion

stages.name: Guardian of Justice
stages.desc:
The wandering warrior has been awaiting this match for over a decade, until a young child comes over: 'You can stop waiting now, that guy died ten years ago. Look, I've been looking for you so long, I'm big now.' Once done speaking, the child disappears, leaving behind only a saber on the ground.
 that deal Arts damage to enemies and heal allies when captured are present on the field

stages.name: Daylight Robbery
stages.desc: 'Jianghu rules: if you beat the two of us, you'll keep all the belongings.' The twin bandits are beacons of righteousness, trying to cover for their comrades as they sneak away.

stages.name: Rescue Mission
stages.desc:
There will always be some tourists who decide they don't want to leave yet when the Garden closes. Not only do you have to see them out, you also need to resolve whatever chaos they caused.
 Blocks the movement of Ursus Civilians and Talulah, the Fighter; Can be destroyed by allied units.
 Depletes a Life Point when killed by an enemy.

stages.name: Toy Soldiers
stages.desc: Once, a foreign guest left behind a set of action figures in the Garden. Now, they have made a place for themselves here.

stages.name: Hook, Line, and Sinker
stages.desc: Only in the Garden of Grotesqueries can you see these extinct Pseudomuts. Enticed into the Garden by their rapacious appetite, they have not had to worry about food or drink for many years since. Both Yi and the Pseudomuts believe they have the favorable side of the arrangement.

stages.name: Stopping the Advance
stages.desc: There's barely any space here to turn your head, let alone your body. Beasts intent on chasing their tails have no choice but to keep running forward, smashing everything in their path. The Statuegeists watching from the sidelines chortle as they pass.

stages.name: Feng Shui
stages.desc:
The geists are alarmed, and they scatter all over the place. Have a closer look. I'll get straight to the point, the road's so curved, not even Originium slugs can stand it! Do some rearranging to get the feng shui right for them.
 that can block the enemy's route can be deployed

stages.name: Away, Hui!
stages.desc:
Garden rules state that at the start of every month, fire will be used to 'drive away the darkness of Hui'. As for what is being burned as fuel, best not ask.
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path.

stages.name: Return to Your Roots
stages.desc:
Time flows, the land changes, and cities of steel rise. Their hometowns are long gone, but they are always looking out for a way back home.
 Blocks the vision of your units

stages.name: Repel the Outsiders
stages.desc: Many people will spend their entire lives never having left their homeland, but they will, and they will put their all into doing so.

stages.name: Shanhaizhong Scramble
stages.desc:
'Of the eight 'Do Nots', the Shanhaizhong intruding in the Garden have violated four of them, so the roads they walk and the searches they perform are all fruitless as the geists 'teach them the rules'. Give them a hand before you hand them over to the Sui Regulator.
 are present on the field that Block enemies

stages.name: Sugared Bait
stages.desc:
After trying to feed the fins in the pond, some tourists were knocked eight zhang away! In order to satisfy those fins, they learned from their mistakes and reflected on the past... deciding to do all they can to develop bigger, sweeter bait.
<'Gelato Stops'> are present on the field that restore allied SP when captured
 are present on the field that Block enemies

stages.name: Cultured
stages.desc: You see that the Seaborn here are daubing paint, letting you breathe a sigh of relief. Then you see, painted on the wall, a 'sea with plenty of'—scrawled, teeming, and diverse 'fins'.

stages.name: Take the Stage
stages.desc: Even though this is no longer an age when many watch plays, they still stubbornly cling to the idea that they have parts to play upon the stage. Some geists are trapped in the past, much like humanity.

stages.name: Exemplary Service
stages.desc:
An armor-clad eccentric sleeps atop the mount, unmoving for over a decade. As you pass them, you suddenly sense yellow sands fill the air, and when you open your eyes, you find yourself before a city gate of steel. Soldiers line the walls while battle drums shake the heavens. Glory days of past service can be both a dream, and a cage.
 are present on the field that Block enemies

stages.name: Beware the Shadows
stages.desc: Two roads, one on either side, both with 'safety instructions'. The obvious dangers mentioned seem easy to guard against, but one line is worrying: Beware the ■■■■ in the shadows. The text is smudged, and hard to read.

stages.name: Let It All Out
stages.desc: Yi has some regrets about letting the geists read the Higashinese novels in his collection. The geists playing ninja had only just scratched the surface of the culture before going on a rampage, causing much more of an uproar than those noisy musicians.

stages.name: Green Hills Speak Not
stages.desc: 'You're sitting on me.' There don't seem to be any of Yi's relics here, but there are plenty of rocks. It is a good time to rest. 'I said, you're sitting on me!' The voice rings out again. You look all around you to find it, until the boulder under you rises into your field of view.

stages.name: Departing Inspection
stages.desc: The Sui Regulator performs additional inspections on visitors leaving forbidden areas, to ensure you are still you, and all that leaves is you.

stages.name: A Humble Gift
stages.desc: A younger brother receiving goods from his older brother is naturally not an issue. However, since one is named Yi and the other Ji, the Candleholders never lack reasons to inspect things being sent up the mountain.

stages.name: Muted Past
stages.desc: Yi was given a worn-out guitar that couldn't produce a single note, but in his hands, it was able to play the most basic of tunes. The Sarkaz, wanting to snatch back this melody, chased him all the way back to the Garden from the west.

stages.name: Mangshan Town Records
stages.desc: 'A horde of wares... awakened... attacked us in the streets... disaster... destruction...' The rest is hidden below an old, dark-red stain, unable to be read clearly.

stages.name: Unlit Sparks
stages.desc:
Here lie abominations born of the food and drink people discard. Yi did not gather them for his collection. If Vapidities can become Sapidities, then there is the possibility they can yet bloom with flavor.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.

stages.name: Honed Edge
stages.desc: Through conservation treatments, items in the collection show their true worth. These particular pieces require a honing of their bravery and valor in endless battle to stay their best.

stages.name: Inferno
stages.desc:
One burning vein of ore stretches on, hiding in Catastrophe. The other awakens evil beasts under the earth from their shallow sleep. Battle Heaven for time, battle the earth for life.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.

stages.name: Garrison
stages.desc:
'Atop the Tomb of Sui, a town was founded to receive guests. Suppressed by the geomantic fortunes of the eight directions, only a single gate was left open for the rite that saps Its strength.'
 Blocks the enemy's line of sight, preventing direct shots.

stages.name: Beyond Mount and Sea
stages.desc: History has been enticed by the present, revealing a past formed from both old and new.

stages.name: Force Redirection
stages.desc:
Use the ways of beasts to reign over beasts. This was Great Yan's most important discovery when fighting against the gods.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.

stages.name: Steady Stream
stages.desc:
The food that Bear Lord's throwing away is attracting Originium slugs! Quick, send someone to tell him: NO LITTERING IN THE GARDEN OF GROTESQUERIES!
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path
 that deal Arts damage to enemies and heal allies when captured are present on the field
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and HP; certain enemies have further increased ATK and HP; 'Gramophone' SP requirement increased

stages.name: Cliff of Geists
stages.desc: Those passing through this natural redoubt always fly into a raging fury, refusing to fall back. Little do they know of the geists who lost their footing and slipped off the precipices on either side. This unnatural anger is their masterpiece.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and HP

stages.name: Sparkling Shine
stages.desc: Don't think you can hide behind those slugs to escape! Get that dog!
stages.eliteDesc: Crying Thief gains greatly increased HP; his attacks will Stun allied units for longer

stages.name: Reeducation
stages.desc: Trading without a license is strictly prohibited in the Garden of Grotesqueries. Get that rat to make an example out of him.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and greatly increased HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Duck and Yi
stages.desc: This duck stocking the shelves definitely made some kind of shady deal with Yi! Get him to 'fess up!
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and HP; allied units have increased Redeployment Time; different enemies will appear

stages.name: Calamitous Punishment
stages.desc:
You have disturbed the Bodhisattva's contemplation, thus you shall be punished. No doubt by experiencing this torment, you shall know the consequences of itchy hands and restless feet.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and greatly increased HP; enemies will follow different routes

stages.name: Of Hundred Forms
stages.desc: When among civilization, Cannot shall treat you in a civilized manner. But if you insist on seeing another side of him, he shall grant your request. Reluctantly.
stages.eliteDesc: Null

stages.name: Feast of Fruit
stages.desc:
Fruit born from the soul of the land is stored here, guarded by rows of man-eating plants. They do not await a farmer to tend them, but rather, you.
 Cannot be attacked, and blocks the vision of your units
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and DEF and greatly increased Max HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Biding Time
stages.desc: When on reconnaissance missions, Raidian will deploy 'lookouts' to establish contact with the main force. There is no information that can escape her net.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP

stages.name: Joy From Burdenbeast
stages.desc: A game about growth. The 'strategist' says growth is like a burdenbeast's plodding steps, gentle and enduring.
stages.eliteDesc: Null

stages.name: Silicon Geist's Banquet
stages.desc: A game about propagation. The 'strategist' says propagation is like man-made objects becoming geists. You can try as hard as you like, but the results are hard to predict.
stages.eliteDesc: Condition: Enemies have increased ATK and HP, and greatly increased DEF and RES

stages.name: Totally Out of Control
stages.desc: A game about continuation. The 'strategist' says continuation is like the paradox of the Infected. To live, one cannot avoid self-sacrifice.
stages.eliteDesc: Condition: Enemies have significantly increased ATK, DEF, HP, ASPD, and Movement Speed

stages.name: Whack-A-Mus
stages.desc: Legend says the musbeasts of the Garden of Grotesqueries love to nibble on ancient tomes of poetry. Visitors who recite sordid poems or vulgar words will hear jeering chitters from the shadows, and chase after them to hidden burrows, ultimately provoking furious battles.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.desc: Occasionally, a group of people will line up in squares in an empty space. When asked, they say: 'We're going to market.' With the strike of a gong, they head off on a tottering march, destroying all that blocks the way. A startling sight for onlookers.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; different enemies will appear

stages.name: Serious Business
stages.desc:
You can't defend a business with only your mouth. But it does seem like Big Bob has misunderstood why you've come to his shop.

stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK and HP; Big Bob's HP is further increased

stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; certain enemies have further increased HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Take the Stage
stages.desc: Even though this is no longer an age when many watch plays, they still stubbornly cling to the idea that they have parts to play upon the stage. Some geists are trapped in the past, much like humanity.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies will appear; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Exemplary Service
stages.desc:
An armor-clad eccentric sleeps atop the mount, unmoving for over a decade. As you pass them, you suddenly sense yellow sands fill the air, and when you open your eyes, you find yourself before a city gate of steel. Soldiers line the walls while battle drums shake the heavens. Glory days of past service can be both a dream, and a cage.
 are present on the field that Block enemies
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; certain enemies have further increased HP; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Beware the Shadows
stages.desc: Two roads, one on either side, both with 'safety instructions'. The obvious dangers mentioned seem easy to guard against, but one line is worrying: Beware the ■■■■ in the shadows. The text is smudged, and hard to read.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, HP, and Movement Speed; additional enemies will appear; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Let It All Out
stages.desc: Yi has some regrets about letting the geists read the Higashinese novels in his collection. The geists playing ninja had only just scratched the surface of the culture before going on a rampage, causing much more of an uproar than those noisy musicians.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; Suiton Masters gain Invisibility

stages.name: Green Hills Speak Not
stages.desc: 'You're sitting on me.' There don't seem to be any of Yi's relics here, but there are plenty of rocks. It is a good time to rest. 'I said, you're sitting on me!' The voice rings out again. You look all around you to find it, until the boulder under you rises into your field of view.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased DEF and greatly increased ATK and HP; certain enemies have further increased HP; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Departing Inspection
stages.desc: The Sui Regulator performs additional inspections on visitors leaving forbidden areas, to ensure you are still you, and all that leaves is you.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies will appear; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: A Humble Gift
stages.desc: A younger brother receiving goods from his older brother is naturally not an issue. However, since one is named Yi and the other Ji, the Candleholders never lack reasons to inspect things being sent up the mountain.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Muted Past
stages.desc: Yi was given a worn-out guitar that couldn't produce a single note, but in his hands, it was able to play the most basic of tunes. The Sarkaz, wanting to snatch back this melody, chased him all the way back to the Garden from the west.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and Weight and greatly increased HP; additional enemies will appear; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Mangshan Town Records
stages.desc: 'A horde of wares... awakened... attacked us in the streets... disaster... destruction...' The rest is hidden below an old, dark-red stain, unable to be read clearly.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain greatly increased ATK and HP; Statuegeists have further increased HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Unlit Sparks
stages.desc:
Here lie abominations born of the food and drink people discard. Yi did not gather them for his collection. If Vapidities can become Sapidities, then there is the possibility they can yet bloom with flavor.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies and Statuegeists will appear; 'Fasting' is Invisible

stages.name: Honed Edge
stages.desc: Through conservation treatments, items in the collection show their true worth. These particular pieces require a honing of their bravery and valor in endless battle to stay their best.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; Rampaging Abnormal Manglerbeast has further increased DEF and HP, and follows a different route

stages.name: Inferno
stages.desc:
One burning vein of ore stretches on, hiding in Catastrophe. The other awakens evil beasts under the earth from their shallow sleep. Battle Heaven for time, battle the earth for life.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; certain enemies have further increased HP; additional enemies will appear; additional Statuegeists will appear

stages.name: Garrison
stages.desc:
'Atop the Tomb of Sui, a town was founded to receive guests. Suppressed by the geomantic fortunes of the eight directions, only a single gate was left open for the rite that saps Its strength.'
 Blocks the enemy's line of sight, preventing direct shots.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies will appear; some enemies have been changed

stages.name: Beyond Mount and Sea
stages.desc: History has been enticed by the present, revealing a past formed from both old and new.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; some enemies have been changed; Statuegeists have reduced HP

stages.name: Force Redirection
stages.desc:
Use the ways of beasts to reign over beasts. This was Great Yan's most important discovery when fighting against the gods.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.
stages.eliteDesc: Enemies gain increased ATK, DEF, and HP; additional enemies will appear

stages.name: Cold Moonlight
stages.desc:
Anyone who searches for that long won't have a much better temper. The chill, colder than ice, is a reflection of her determination—even though this is only a statue of her.
 Periodically releases a frigid wave that will inflict Cold on your units

stages.name: Defining the World
stages.desc: The limit of what humanity can see, match, or define.

stages.name: Defining the World
stages.desc:
The limit of what humanity can see, match, or define.
'Body' reflects a portion of damage received; all enemies have increased ATK, DEF, and HP, while 'Restrained Pride' and 'Fume of Anger' have further increased Max HP; 'Vessel of Shuo' and 'Arrogance Embodied' use their skills more often

stages.name: Memories of Dusk Beauty
stages.desc: People only ever speak of her searching the twin moons for her husband, but they never understand why she does so. Winter's grip may loosen, bitter frost may thaw, time may pass, but these memories shall never be erased.

stages.name: Sword, Glaive, Spear
stages.desc:
They died before their lofty ambitions could be realized. The three heroes' weapons have faced the arms and armor of the Hundred Clans. If they saw Yan as it is today, would they know contentment?
 Operators deployed on it and enemies who have passed it will take constant damage, but their ATK and ASPD will be increased significantly.

stages.name: Compassion, Justice, Valor
stages.desc: The virtuous begin with compassion. From compassion stems justice. Only in the end do they call for valor. And so, despite failing to quell the unrest under heaven, they shall be remembered with kind words for generations to come.

stages.name: Enlightenment
stages.desc: Here, where the clouds lie deep, your footsteps alarm the pondfins. Before you lies a thousand years of Yan. Under your feet rests ten thousand years of the land. The elderly man sits upright as he places his finning rod down, waiting for the opportune one to step forward and talk.

stages.name: Seeking the Way
stages.desc: After experiencing a lifetime of strife, he retreated beyond the land, free, unfettered. The one who has come to talk has witnessed breeze and drizzle, but naturally must also witness gale and storm.

stages.name: The Suppression of Sui
stages.desc:
All things may act as the Garden of Grotesqueries' beams and columns, but there is only one who can bear the weight of it, only one who can bestow order upon it. It is not ye, but Yi. Sway his heart, and Yan shall breathe easy.
 Operators deployed on it will earn a Hui-Ming Attribute.

stages.name: A Change of Fate
stages.desc:
Yi is more than happy to allow you in, but ere you take another step, you must complete your duty.
 Operators deployed on it gain a Hui-Ming Attribute.

stages.name: Words Past Like Smoke
stages.desc: What caused the bestial Sui to reawaken, then leave no trace upon the mortal realm after Its destruction?

stages.name: Near-Forgotten
stages.desc: When an uninkable scroll meets an unwritable past.

stages.name: The Sui Strategist
stages.desc: Rather than fooling around with ceremonies year after year, it would be better to just pound the body of Sui into the dust to create a lasting peace. There is a 'man' who planned to do as such, and he approves of your gumption, but he cannot forgive you disturbing his kin's eternal rest.

stages.name: The Last Hunt
stages.desc: The clearest recollection of the war in Sui's memories. It will know victory, and It will know defeat.

stages.name: Recorded Memory
stages.desc: The memories of the Sui proxy induce signs of geistification in the ink-turned-icon, but the finishing touch still requires a human hand.

stages.name: Stasis
stages.desc:
Let unending evolution end here.
'Body' reflects a portion of damage received; 'Restrained Pride' and 'Fume of Anger' have increased Max HP

stages.desc:

stages.name: Astral Projection
stages.desc: Why is this good merchant in your dream? Is he here to give you a treasure or two? Why isn't he happy to accept these presents you offer him?

stages.name: Sit Back and Watch
stages.desc: He's thought of a lot of ways to handle your unruliness. His latest idea is what he calls 'fighting fire with fire'. Besides, the goods are lost either way, so why not sit back and enjoy a cup of tea while watching you tear at each other's throats?

stages.name: Steady Stream
stages.desc:
The food that Bear Lord's throwing away is attracting Originium slugs! Quick, send someone to tell him: NO LITTERING IN THE GARDEN OF GROTESQUERIES!
This stage has  that can block the enemy's path
 that deal Arts damage to enemies and heal allies when captured are present on the field

stages.name: Cliff of Geists
stages.desc: Those passing through this natural redoubt always fly into a raging fury, refusing to fall back. Little do they know of the geists who lost their footing and slipped off the precipices on either side. This unnatural anger is their masterpiece.

stages.name: Sparkling Shine
stages.desc: Don't think you can hide behind those slugs to escape! Get that dog!

stages.name: Reeducation
stages.desc: Trading without a license is strictly prohibited in the Garden of Grotesqueries. Get that rat to make an example out of him.

stages.name: Duck and Yi
stages.desc: This duck stocking the shelves definitely made some kind of shady deal with Yi! Get him to 'fess up!

stages.name: Calamitous Punishment
stages.desc:
You have disturbed the Bodhisattva's contemplation, thus you shall be punished. No doubt by experiencing this torment, you shall know the consequences of itchy hands and restless feet.
 Summons 'Sapid Auspice' to deal True damage to all enemies in its path.
 Summons 'Vapid Blight' to deal True damage to all allied units in its path.

stages.name: Of Hundred Forms
stages.desc: When among civilization, Cannot shall treat you in a civilized manner. But if you insist on seeing another side of him, he shall grant your request. Reluctantly.

stages.name: Feast of Fruit
stages.desc:
Fruit born from the soul of the land is stored here, guarded by rows of man-eating plants. They do not await a farmer to tend them, but rather, you.
 Cannot be attacked, and blocks the vision of your units

stages.name: Biding Time
stages.desc: When on reconnaissance missions, Raidian will deploy 'lookouts' to establish contact with the main force. There is no information that can escape her net.

stages.name: Joy From Burdenbeast
stages.desc: A game about growth. The 'strategist' says growth is like a burdenbeast's plodding steps, gentle and enduring.

stages.name: Silicon Geist's Banquet
stages.desc: A game about propagation. The 'strategist' says propagation is like man-made objects becoming geists. You can try as hard as you like, but the results are hard to predict.

stages.name: Totally Out of Control
stages.desc: A game about continuation. The 'strategist' says continuation is like the paradox of the Infected. To live, one cannot avoid self-sacrifice.

stages.name: Bout by the Dusk River
stages.desc:
Since needless arguing will lead to a flurry of blows, why not just get straight to the punching and kicking?
Use Ranged Operators to fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Bout by the Dusk River
stages.desc:
Since needless arguing will lead to a flurry of blows, why not just get straight to the punching and kicking?
Use Melee Operators to fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Heroes of Nanwu
stages.desc:
Taking first place is only the prelude to this gathering of the jianghu. Why do you think the Nanwu styles boast so many martial schools and escort agencies?
Use Ranged Operators to fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Heroes of Nanwu
stages.desc:
Taking first place is only the prelude to this gathering of the jianghu. Why do you think the Nanwu styles boast so many martial schools and escort agencies?
Use Ranged and Melee Operators to fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Dusk River Sayings
stages.desc:
Having come to this, life and death are decided.
Fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Nanwu Vows
stages.desc:
Vows have been sworn. All that is left is to triumph.
Fight the foe you picked; Losing will not deduct Life Points. Defeating a tougher foe within the given time grants a better reward

stages.name: Clarity
stages.desc: Someone has placed a board here where black and white intersect, so that you will not be confused when you sit down for an actual game.

stages.name: Slandering Heaven
stages.desc:
If Heaven does not stand with you, snatch it yourself.
Candle Stand: Slandering Heaven: the Operators on this stand and the four adjacent tiles take progressively higher amounts of Arts damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Slandering Heaven
stages.desc:

Candle Stand: Slandering Heaven: the Operators on this stand and the four adjacent tiles take progressively higher amounts of Arts damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Amidst Thunder
stages.desc:
If storm clouds gather, take it yourself.
Candle Stand: Amidst Thunder: the Operator on this stand takes a proportion of their Max HP as True damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Amidst Thunder
stages.desc:
If storm clouds gather, take it yourself.
Candle Stand: Amidst Thunder: the Operator on this stand takes a proportion of their Max HP as True damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Defying Quakes
stages.desc:
If the earth spawns disaster, remove it yourself.
Candle Stand: Defying Quakes: the Operator on this stand takes a proportion of their ATK as Physical damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Defying Quakes

Candle Stand: Defying Quakes: the Operator on this stand takes a proportion of their ATK as Physical damage at regular intervals. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: To the Fall
stages.desc:
If Originium falls, calm it yourself.
Candle Stand: To the Fall: the Operator on this stand takes several instances of Arts damage at regular intervals. For every 4 Originium Ingots owned, number of instances +1. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: To the Fall
stages.desc:

Candle Stand: To the Fall: the Operator on this stand takes several instances of Arts damage at regular intervals. For every 4 Originium Ingots owned, number of instances +1. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Against the Flood
stages.desc:
If water flows wild, redirect it yourself.
Candle Stand: Against the Flood: the Operator on this stand is more likely to be attacked by enemies, and receives increased Physical and Arts damage. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: Against the Flood
stages.desc:
If water flows wild, redirect it yourself.
Candle Stand: Against the Flood: the Operator on this stand is more likely to be attacked by enemies, and receives increased Physical and Arts damage. If the battle ends without that Operator retreating, they become a Candleguest.

stages.name: The Four Difficulties
stages.desc: To stand out from the crowd is a difficulty. To apply what you have learned is a difficulty. To thrive is a difficulty. To free yourself from wallowing is a difficulty.

stages.name: Confusion
stages.desc:
Do not get ensnared by the web. Be decisive, and follow through.
Obtain Collectibles and other rewards upon victory

stages.name: Difficult Beginnings
stages.desc: Thunder claps, and the maelstrom stops.

stages.name: Everywhere
stages.desc: Seek similarity, reject difference.

stages.name: Sealed Time
stages.desc: Time has frozen this herd in a moment of confusion, but all seals must one day break. Best get out of here quick.

stages.name: Omnipresent Gospel
stages.desc: No matter who collected them or where they are placed, the pervasive radiance of the holy statues of Laterano cannot be concealed.

stages.name: Conformity
stages.desc: On the left walk men, on the right, geists. From afar, they fire. When near, they strike. In the end, they come together as one.

enemy_2001_duckmi.name: Duck Lord
enemy_2001_duckmi.desc: A famous Columbian investor, entrepreneur, and master of 'Duck Power.' Out of the public eye, enjoys kicking back with others...
enemy_2001_duckmi.ability:
Cannot be blocked.
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after being attacked

enemy_2002_bearmi.name: Gopnik
enemy_2002_bearmi.desc: The Ursus security guard hired by Duck Lord. Powerful, but not very smart. Mainly responsible for carrying luggage for Duck Lord.

enemy_2034_sythef.name: Crying Thief
enemy_2034_sythef.desc: A Perro who left his family to seek out a new life... but all that people know is that he is always weeping.

enemy_2085_skzjxd.name: Fatty
enemy_2085_skzjxd.desc: Duck Lord's associate and apprentice, an opportunist with a slick tongue. Frequently appears on dubious TV shows, preaching about how he cares little for losses or gains, as they are part of the natural money-making process.
enemy_2085_skzjxd.ability:
Always faces the direction with the most Operators.
Takes 80% less Physical/Arts damage from the front.

enemy_1396_dhdts.name: Fieldmus Bruiser
enemy_1396_dhdts.desc: The more bountiful the harvest, the fatter the fieldmus. When these creatures grow four-packs, even the Tianshis sigh—for they destroy Throttle Valves with frightening efficiency.

enemy_2101_dyspll.name: Talisman

enemy_2106_dyremy.name: Strange Gourd

enemy_2119_dyshhj_2.name: 'Body of Sui'

enemy_1403_gunsho.name: Light Rapid Crossbowman

enemy_1401_pogpro.name: Coalition Army Hound
enemy_1401_pogpro.desc: A hound trained by the Bolívar Coalition Army. Perro officers were not too enthused about this being introduced into their tactical considerations.

enemy_1001_bigbo.name: Big Bob
enemy_1001_bigbo.desc: One of Reunion's squad leaders. He wears a heavy blast suit and attacks with an electric chainsaw. He has been spotted leading peripheral operations in some remote areas, and is known to be a seasoned combatant and commander. Due to his devastating attacks and extreme survivability, proceed with utmost caution.

enemy_1007_slime_3.name: Originium Slug β
enemy_1007_slime_3.desc: An infected wild creature that is more dangerous than an Originium Slug α. Due to their low intelligence, they are easily controlled by enemy casters for use as cannon fodder during invasions.

enemy_1010_demon.name: Sarkaz Greatswordsman
enemy_1010_demon.desc: A mercenary from Sarkaz. The Originium inside them has boosted their physical abilities, allowing them to wield larger weapons with ease and withstand more damage.

enemy_2102_dytmbr.name: Shadow Scholar
enemy_2102_dytmbr.desc: A character often seen in Yanese shadow plays, prone to strange encounters in those plays. This Shadowgeist has seen these scenes play out so many times that it believes them to be real, and even warning of the talismans is not enough to dissuade it.

enemy_1123_spsbr.name: 'Bitey'
enemy_1123_spsbr.desc: A common Ink Spirit named by the painter herself. It's very smart and actually understands human speech. Apparently some kind of communal organism, it gets lonely when left by itself. It has done many a mean thing out of amusement for it's just an Ink Spirit, no matter how smart.

enemy_10007_trspsb.name: 'Ink Brew'
enemy_10007_trspsb.desc: A carefree Ink Spirit named by the painter herself. An inebriating aroma wafts from it, perhaps due to wine mixed into the ink—a joke from an old friend with a fondness for drink.
enemy_10007_trspsb.ability:
Non-Leader enemies passing by this enemy gain bonus ASPD and Movement Speed, and take less Physical and Arts damage.
Really likes .

enemy_10065_ftzlc.name: Cabbage Seedling
enemy_10065_ftzlc.desc: A small, common fae, the original form of Patrolling Foliage. Completely harmless if separated from fire.

enemy_10064_ftyzss.name: Patrolling Foliage

enemy_10071_ftprg.name: 'End Point'
enemy_10071_ftprg.desc: A fae still learning how to trick people. It disguises itself as the end of the path, or the shape of a cunning tuskbeast. However, it usually misses some crucial detail.

enemy_2125_dylnpp.name: Little Ba'jie

One of the few geists not under the supervision of the Sui Regulator or Tianshi Bureaus. It spends its days interacting with visitors at the souvenir shop, or playing with ritual processions on the days when the garden is closed.

enemy_1099_nbkght.desc: An independent Kazimierz knight. A low profile and unrefined skill means this one will soon be swept away in the tide of knight competition.

enemy_1103_wdkght_2.name: Bloodboil Knightclub Elite
enemy_1103_wdkght_2.desc: A regular knight from the Bloodboil Knightclub. The Bloodboil Knights have long been known for their ferocity and aggression, from trainees to knight nobles. Whenever one of their brothers falls, they gain courage and combat prowess.
enemy_1103_wdkght_2.ability: Each time an enemy is defeated, gains ATK and ASPD, stacking up to 10 times.

enemy_1095_ccripr.name: Tiacauh Fanatic
enemy_1095_ccripr.desc: A fervent Tiacauh warrior with very fast attack speed. Though each strike is not particularly powerful, those caught off guard will be quickly subdued by a rain of blows.
enemy_1095_ccripr.ability: Every attack reduces the enemy's DEF

enemy_2104_dycast.name: Mirror Tianshi
enemy_2104_dycast.desc: A well-polished mirror that believes it sees everything clearly. When it became a Geist, it was not content with just seeing things, but could not stop itself from giving unsolicited advice, just like the old scholars in the Tianshi Bureau.
enemy_2104_dycast.ability: Picks a target from very long range, dealing Arts damage and sending a

enemy_1195_sfyin.name: Grindstone
enemy_1195_sfyin.desc: A Waregeist that manifested when it was infected by Sui-Xiang. All blades must be sharpened to not lose their edge. A Waregeist of this type tends to be timid and will dash out of harm's way at the first sign of danger.

enemy_1304_mhwolf.name: Oxmane
enemy_1304_mhwolf.desc: A carnivore common to the mountains that hunts primarily in packs. Bearing developed mandibles, they crush their prey's skulls with ease. Most fond of feeding on hornbeast stragglers.

enemy_1309_mhboar.name: Direswine
enemy_1309_mhboar.desc: A robustly built omnivore, with an impenetrable epidermis leaving them with few natural predators. Violent headbutts are both their method of attack and a habit necessary for suppressing the growth of their fangs.

enemy_2103_dykens.name: Bottle Swordsman
enemy_2103_dykens.desc: Once a wandering physician's medicine bottle, it provided healing to many warriors. When it became a geist, it was moved by a talisman and put the talisman in its bottle, embarking on a journey of chivalry.

enemy_1088_ltsmer.name: Leithanian Whisperer
enemy_1088_ltsmer.desc: An Arts user commonly seen throughout Leithanien. They attack using Arts, and will cast on L-44 'Gramophones' within their attack range to attempt to seize control of them.

enemy_10048_prtsld.name: Bloody Chef

enemy_10043_sailor.name: Risk-seeking Sailor

enemy_1144_merlgd.name: War Phantom

enemy_1327_cbrokt.name: Arc Commando

Once, someone announced they'd successfully tamed one of these creatures; afterwards, they were never seen again.

enemy_1195_sfyin_2.name: Brightmirror
enemy_1195_sfyin_2.desc: A Waregeist that manifested when it was infected by Sui-Xiang. All blades must be sharpened to not lose their edge. A Waregeist of this type tends to be timid, but often goes the extra mile in an attempt to prove itself.

enemy_1201_sfzhi.name: Inkwave
enemy_1201_sfzhi.desc: A Waregeist that manifested when it was infected by Sui-Xiang. It is always difficult to put brush to paper, and though the paperweight was once calm and patient, it is now anxious and restless.

enemy_1201_sfzhi_2.name: Heartbillow

enemy_1176_dusocr.name: Dublinn Evocator
enemy_1176_dusocr.desc: An evocation caster in the Dublinn forces who can control fireballs. Their leader's teachings reverberate in their minds as they work to purify the corruption of Terra in raging flames.

enemy_1173_duspfr.name: Dublinn Flamerazer
enemy_1173_duspfr.desc: A special squad that uses flamethrowers in the Dublinn forces. Their leader's teachings reverberate in their minds as they seek to reforge Terra amidst the endless flames.

enemy_1166_dusbr.name: Dublinn Scout
enemy_1166_dusbr.desc: A scout for the Dublinn forces. With superb concealment techniques, they are the cornerstone of the Dublinn forces' guerilla maneuvers. Equipped with a refractive mask crafted by Shadowcasters, they are rather resistant to regular Originium Arts.

enemy_1165_duhond.name: Dublinn Sniffer Hound
enemy_1165_duhond.desc: An organism controlled by the Dublinn forces. They were originally purchased from a mercenary group. Equipped with a refractive mask crafted by Shadowcasters, they are rather resistant to regular Originium Arts.

enemy_10069_ftlsj.name: Tree Shield
enemy_10069_ftlsj.desc: Stubborn fae that took root, gazing upon the days and nights of travelers straying into the Dark Woods with no way of escape.

enemy_10066_ftxjl.name: 'Nest'

enemy_10122_uacann_2.name: Army Heavy Artillery
enemy_10122_uacann_2.desc: Heavy artillery manufactured by the Ursus Army, first revealed at the Grypherburg Grand Parade five years ago. Rumors say that a single shell from one can engulf an entire city block in flames, but the number of units in service remains unknown.

enemy_10124_uashld.name: Army Shieldguard
enemy_10124_uashld.desc: Shieldguards of the Ursus Army. In the distance, a faint black line presses slowly forward. No one knows what their massive shields signify, only that screams keep coming from the cloud of dust in their wake.

enemy_3010_mcreep.name: Miner Guerrilla
enemy_3010_mcreep.desc: Resistance unit formed by miners, many of whom are still relatively young. They are not a rampaging mob: all they want to do is protect their families who are still underground.
enemy_3010_mcreep.ability: Allied unit; recovers HP when within any Medic Operator's Attack Range

enemy_10120_uaghst.name: Army Infiltrator
enemy_10120_uaghst.desc: Members of the Ursus Infiltrator Squad. They have already infiltrated every corner of the battlefield, waiting for a chance to strike when the main Army arrives.

Cannot be blocked.

enemy_10121_uasnip.name: Army Mobile Crossbowman
enemy_10121_uasnip.desc: Mobile crossbowmen of the Ursus Army, pushing on the flanks to disrupt and contain the enemy. Cheap to recruit and train, they are often deployed in large numbers.

enemy_10126_rkbomb.name: Abnormal Originium Slug
enemy_10126_rkbomb.desc: These Originium slugs have mutated due to the environmental changes in the mines, caused by an unknown substance. Approach with care, as they are highly unstable.

enemy_1098_cchmpn.name: Tiacauh Brave
enemy_1098_cchmpn.desc: A very formidable Tiacauh warrior, naturally born to do battle. These warriors harbor great pride in their combat abilities, and live for the thrill of the duel no matter where they are. Their single-mindedness allows them to shrug off all outside interference.

enemy_1098_cchmpn_2.name: Tiacauh Champion

These warriors harbor great pride in their combat abilities, and live for the thrill of the duel no matter where they are. Their single-mindedness allows them to shrug off all outside interference.
enemy_1098_cchmpn_2.ability: When blocked, this unit will only receive damage from the unit blocking it, and the DEF of both units is decreased.

enemy_1295_ymph_2.name: Raging Di Beast
enemy_1295_ymph_2.desc: One of Ya's off-handed creations, its bloodlust honed by the golden sands. It is extremely keen of hearing and stalks the streets like a shadow, both fighting and collecting and relaying information.

enemy_1298_ymmonk_2.name: Shanhaizhong Phalanx
enemy_1298_ymmonk_2.desc: A Shanhaizhong member tasked with defense. The Shanhaizhong have recruited many desperadoes, many of them were the disasters of their places. With their burly physiques and special heavy shields, they are a threatening line of defense.

enemy_1299_ymkilr.name: Shanhaizhong Ringleader
enemy_1299_ymkilr.desc: A Shanhaizhong leader hidden in their ranks. Elusive and unflinchingly cruel, are these acts of 'faith' delusional or simply selfish?

enemy_10100_crrol_2.name: Sentient Mannequin
enemy_10100_crrol_2.desc: A mannequin that nimbly darts through the set. Its duty is to transport props. It will do so even when uncommanded, and it will disembowel anyone who stands in its way.

enemy_1304_mhwolf_2.name: Keenfanged Oxmane

enemy_1321_wdarft_2.desc: An organic construct containing the Nachzehrer King's Originium Arts. It can extract all kinds of dead souls from the battlefield to create Seeds of Decay.

enemy_1041_lazerd_2.name: Arts Master A2
enemy_1041_lazerd_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that had some of its Flight Support modules swapped out, trading speed for a more powerful Arts Weapon module. Capable of using long-range Arts attacks. Proceed with caution. Based on available data, its weapon module also allows it to block some Arts damage.

enemy_1042_frostd.name: Frost
enemy_1042_frostd.desc: An unmanned enemy drone equipped with heavy armor to make it harder to destroy. Its prototype was designed by a graphics engine studio. They are equipped with a special device that allows them to drastically reduce the temperature around them, greatly decreasing the attack speed of nearby Operators.

Flying unit.

enemy_1101_plkght_2.name: Roar Knightclub Elite
enemy_1101_plkght_2.desc: Wearing the high-performance, lightweight armor provided by Roar Guards, this new batch of experimental armor has some humorous deficiencies, and even though these are battle-hardened knights, they have still been reduced to pitiful test subjects. 'Roar Guards, even the winds will bow to you!'
enemy_1101_plkght_2.ability: Has significantly increased DEF and RES for a period of time

enemy_1405_boomer.name: Reckless Grenadier
enemy_1405_boomer.desc: A specialized combatant who does not carry conventional weapons. Though well-paid and exempted from standard military training, nobody envies them.

enemy_1269_nhfly_2.name: Seed of Decay

enemy_10027_vtsk.name: 'Armor of the Empire'

enemy_1062_rager.name: Enraged Possessed Soldier

enemy_1291_dusrch.name: Swamp Rover
enemy_1291_dusrch.desc: A military vehicle capable of traveling smoothly through swampy terrain, fitted with advanced detection equipment.
enemy_1291_dusrch.ability:
Immune to the effects of Mire
Reveals Camouflaged and Invisible allied units within range

enemy_1260_durmag_2.name: Sandcastle Architect
enemy_1260_durmag_2.desc: A Durin who has made a career out of designing sandcastles. Having accepted the reality that it is impossible to actually build a sandcastle, he has not even been drafting designs recently, instead focusing his efforts on tinkering with the Arts Unit.

enemy_1338_bhrjst_2.name: Elite Wasteland Skulker
enemy_1338_bhrjst_2.desc: An elite member of a bandit gang operating in the wastelands, sneakily operating in the shadows. Some claim to have complicated pasts, but the truth of it is irrelevant: their skillsets are now used only for survival.
enemy_1338_bhrjst_2.ability:
Can appear in the battlefield
Invisible
When on the field, natural DP regeneration rate is reduced

enemy_1341_bthtms_2.name: Bounty Hunter Disruptor Captain
enemy_1341_bthtms_2.desc: A bounty hunter who uses melee weapons and Originium Arts to intimidate others. New bounties don't show up every day, so why not earn some extra money in their free time?
enemy_1341_bthtms_2.ability:
Can appear in the battlefield
Deals Arts damage
Every Disruptor Captain on the field will reduce Max DP by a certain amount

enemy_10089_hlsprt_2.name: Veteran Shrine Nurturer
enemy_10089_hlsprt_2.desc: Nurturers of the Shrine of Nurtury selected to ensure that the Nativity goes smoothly. They listen to the hearts of pregnant mothers and comfort newly born infants. They mourn every death and give everything for the newborns of Laterano.
enemy_10089_hlsprt_2.ability: Periodically restores HP and reloads 1 ammo for 5 nearby enemies

enemy_1171_durokt_2.name: Dublinn Flying Squad Leader
enemy_1171_durokt_2.desc: A flying squad leader in the Dublinn forces. The steam propulsion device they use originates from a secret laboratory owned by the Victorian military and has gone through many iterations. Its power output is relatively stable.

enemy_1040_bombd_2.name: Bombtail-G
enemy_1040_bombd_2.desc: An unmanned enemy drone that flies slowly. They carry a large explosive warhead that is launched when approaching nearby Operators, inflicting physical damage in an area. Their design seems to have been inspired by a fantasy creature. Though they cannot attack anymore after launching the warhead, their movement speed increases afterwards due to the loss of weight.
enemy_1040_bombd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Can launch a warhead to deal AoE physical damage
Gains significantly increased Movement Speed after launching warhead

enemy_10091_hlsttu_2.name: Praying Saint Statue
enemy_10091_hlsttu_2.desc: A saint's statue near the Shrine of Nurtury, modified to ensure that the Nativity goes smoothly. 'May the Law and punishment be with you.'
enemy_10091_hlsttu_2.ability: Enemies within range gain a Barrier proportionate to their Max HP every second (capped)

enemy_10083_hlbird_2.name: 'Flight of the Sankta'
enemy_10083_hlbird_2.desc: Haloed fowlbeast that the Law has determined to be Sankta. They have learned to pray, and gotten used to the 'sun' being so close to their heads. They are the aerial extension of faith.
enemy_10083_hlbird_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Gains Fear for several seconds when its HP drops below 50% for the first time

enemy_10084_hlegle_2.name: 'Witness of the Sankta'
enemy_10084_hlegle_2.desc: Mutated haloed fowlbeast that the Law has determined to be Sankta. They have learned to pray and circle above the streets and alleys of the Holy City. They are the Holy City's perception of itself.
enemy_10084_hlegle_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Gains Fear for several seconds when its HP drops below 50% for the first time
Attempts to steal 1 Ammo from the target when attacking, and deals heavy Physical damage if attempt is unsuccessful

enemy_1429_lrwzrd.name: Focused Soul Remnant
enemy_1429_lrwzrd.desc: Soul remnant born of a revenant's will. As it absorbs the roving maelstroms, it releases turbulent witchcraft and emotions that impale enemies both in front and behind.
enemy_1429_lrwzrd.ability:
Attacks deal Arts damage. Can pick up and retain up to 3 Soul Maelstroms.
When carrying a Soul Maelstrom, expends all Soul Maelstroms to deal continuous Arts damage to the target and all your units in a line.

enemy_1428_lrsoul.name: Soul Maelstrom
enemy_1428_lrsoul.desc: Soul fragments born of a revenant's will. Innumerable emotions intertwine within—millennia of confused memories converging into a tangible maelstrom.

enemy_1429_lrwzrd_2.name: Prism Soul Remnant
enemy_1429_lrwzrd_2.desc: Powerful soul remnant born of a revenant's will. As it absorbs roving maelstroms, it releases violent witchcraft and emotions that impale enemies both in front and behind.
enemy_1429_lrwzrd_2.ability:
Attacks deal Arts damage. Can pick up and retain up to 3 Soul Maelstroms.
When carrying a Soul Maelstrom, expends all Soul Maelstroms to deal continuous Arts damage to the target and all your units in a line.

enemy_1216_cansld_2.name: Pathfinder Ballistarius Prefect
enemy_1216_cansld_2.desc: A stalwart warrior who rushes forward on the front lines, firing shells capable of inflicting widespread destruction. By virtue of their physique, they can withstand the recoil from their cannons, and are proficient in close combat even without their weapons. Extremely reliable.
enemy_1216_cansld_2.ability:
While this unit is holding Ammo, attacks become long-ranged and deal Splash Damage. Ammo capacity: 2
Deals additional Arts damage to 'Gelato Stops'

enemy_1256_lyacpa_2.name: R-11a Assault Power Armor
enemy_1256_lyacpa_2.desc: A model equipped with long-ranged weapons, massively increasing its offensive capabilities. It's difficult to imagine what sort of changes might come about if this tech could be mass-produced.

enemy_1254_lypa_2.name: Test Power Armor
enemy_1254_lypa_2.desc: An unpiloted Power Armor. It seems to have a mind of its own, and even experienced engineers struggle to tell if it's being piloted.

enemy_1105_tyokai_2.name: Luxury Sponsor Drone
enemy_1105_tyokai_2.desc: Ellen Select Technology thanks you for your generous sponsorship! This heavy and luxurious drone carries the passion of the fans, bringing anything to the knights on the field: devices, fincatching rods, or carbonated beverages! The outcome of the battle is in your hands!
enemy_1105_tyokai_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Does not count towards the defeated enemies counter
When destroyed, grants a large number of deployable units

enemy_1106_byokai.name: Cheering Sponsor Drone
enemy_1106_byokai.desc: Ellen Select Technology thanks you for your heartfelt sponsorship! This drone broadcasts select commercials from the General Chamber of Commerce to knights and spectators at all times, stimulating the crowd and making the competition that much more exciting!
enemy_1106_byokai.ability:
Flying unit.
Does not count towards the defeated enemies counter
Upon death, significantly increase ASPD and DEF of our units on the field for a period.

enemy_10083_hlbird.name: 'Wings of the Sankta'
enemy_10083_hlbird.desc: Haloed fowlbeast that the Law has determined to be Sankta. They have not yet gotten used to the 'sun' being so close to their heads, so they scatter and flutter in an attempt to shake it off.
enemy_10083_hlbird.ability:
Flying unit.
Gains Fear for several seconds when its HP drops below 50% for the first time

enemy_1144_merlgd_2.name: Legendary War Phantom
enemy_1144_merlgd_2.desc: The 'War Phantoms' were once a legendary band of mercenaries. Though countless others have appropriated their name, perhaps the legends themselves have yet to fall. Extremely strong and adept at controlling multiple types of Air Support Drones.
enemy_1144_merlgd_2.ability:
On first attack, immediately dispatches  to support the battle from the Mobile Tactical Hangar
The first time HP drops below half, immediately dispatches  from the Mobile Tactical Hangar to support the battle
When on the field, all Air Support Drones gain greatly increased Movement Speed

enemy_1145_atkspd_2.name: Scrap Model-U
enemy_1145_atkspd_2.desc: The enemy's advanced offensive Air Support Drones. Its attacks deal stronger AoE Physical damage and reduce the targets' DEF.
enemy_1145_atkspd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Attacks fire two grenades, significantly reducing the DEF of the target and nearby allies for a short period
Difficult to attack
Will automatically retreat some time after launch

enemy_1146_defspd_2.name: Anvil Model-B
enemy_1146_defspd_2.desc: The enemy's advanced defensive Air Support Drones. It greatly strengthens the DEF of enemy units within range and gradually recovers their HP.
enemy_1146_defspd_2.ability:
Flying unit.
Continually provides boosted DEF and HP recovery to nearby enemy units
Difficult to attack
Will automatically retreat some time after launch

enemy_1513_dekght.name: Corrupted Knight
enemy_1513_dekght.desc: A Sarkaz knight with a broken horn who radiates terror and shows clear signs of excessive drug use. Uses a warhammer in battle. Crushes enemies with terrifying bloodlust. When the Withered Knight falls, takes up the quest and becomes dramatically stronger. Every step he takes spreads corruption.
enemy_1513_dekght.ability:
Attacks deal Physical damage to the target and adjacent allied units
Does charged attacks, dealing heavy Physical damage to the targets and any adjacent units
Gains greatly increased ATK, ASPD, and Movement Speed when  dies

enemy_1513_dekght_2.name: Withered Knight
enemy_1513_dekght_2.desc: A Sarkaz knight with a broken horn who radiates terror and shows clear signs of excessive drug use. Uses a cursed bow in battle. Can shoot multiple targets at once and plant seeds of withering arts. When the Corrupted Knight falls, takes up the quest and becomes dramatically stronger. Knights that are hostile to him will shift to Wither.
enemy_1513_dekght_2.ability:
Attacks 2 targets simultaneously
Fires explosive arrows at three targets which explode after a short delay, dealing high Arts damage to the target and surrounding four tiles
Gains greatly increased ATK, ASPD, and Movement Speed when  dies

enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.name: Nova Knightclub Elite
enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.desc: An elite of the Nova Knightclub. With the Candle Knight as its figurehead, this Knightclub with a beautiful name recruits elegant knights proficient in using Arts from far and wide. Many of these elites have achieved great mastery over their Arts, some even plotting to overthrow the Candle Knight.
enemy_1184_cadkgt_2.ability:
[Glimmering Touch] After charging for some time, deals a large amount of Arts damage to a target and all surrounding units
While charging, loses Invisibility

enemy_1118_lidbox_2.name: Elite Pugilist Prisoner
enemy_1118_lidbox_2.desc: An inmate who wholeheartedly trusts in his fists. Do not underestimate this inmate who is able to utilize both fist and chain in harmony now.
enemy_1118_lidbox_2.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and attacks ignore a certain portion of the target's DEF

enemy_1119_vofsd_2.name: Herculean Prisoner
enemy_1119_vofsd_2.desc: An inmate with immense strength. The weapons in his hands seem to have been acquired from ransacking a storage room. During a melee, will often rush to the frontline and inflict great damage.
enemy_1119_vofsd_2.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK and RES and regenerates HP over time

enemy_1121_lifbos_2.name: Infamous Recidivist
enemy_1121_lifbos_2.desc: A legendary prisoner whose might is second only to that of Anthony. Has learned patience after his long stay in prison, and pays no attention to normal attacks and provocations. However, avoid enraging him at all costs, or be prepared to face the consequences.
enemy_1121_lifbos_2.ability:
Starts imprisoned. When imprisoned, ASPD is reduced but gains increased DEF
Freed after a few attacks, gaining increased ATK
When freed for the first time, immediately frees all other enemies

enemy_1190_krgbow_2.name: Icefield Hunter Leader
enemy_1190_krgbow_2.desc: A seasoned hunter living on the icefields. After finding the weakest link in a group of prey, they will hound it relentlessly until one of them falls dead.
enemy_1190_krgbow_2.ability: Prioritizes attacking the target with the lowest DEF

enemy_1213_mtrsnp_2.name: Pathfinder Sagittarius Prefect
enemy_1213_mtrsnp_2.desc: An elite warrior proficient at using guns in long-range combat. Their attacks are precise, and especially deadly when loaded with special ammunition.
enemy_1213_mtrsnp_2.ability: While this unit is holding Ammo, ATK increases. Ammo capacity: 1

enemy_1076_bsthmr.name: Demolitionist
enemy_1076_bsthmr.desc: A high-level elite Reunion combatant who wears heavy-duty, explosion-proof protective gear. Their special Originium-powered hammers have massive destructive range, and must be dealt with cautiously.
enemy_1076_bsthmr.ability: Attacks cause an explosion, dealing Splash damage to units around the primary target.

enemy_1045_hammer.name: Defense Crusher
enemy_1045_hammer.desc: One of Reunion's senior elites, clad in a heavy blast suit. Each swing from their weapon is slow but packs tremendous destructive power. Operators will be stunned after taking several attacks.Proceed with caution.
enemy_1045_hammer.ability: After two attacks, the next attack Stuns its target

enemy_1431_lrmzrd.name: Infused Soul Husk
enemy_1431_lrmzrd.desc: Soul husk born of a revenant's will, imbued with the techniques of countless heroes in its memories. The heroes have all but fallen, leaving behind only indistinct silhouettes as memories.
enemy_1431_lrmzrd.ability:
Can pick up and retain up to 3 Soul Maelstroms.
When carrying a Soul Maelstrom, expends Soul Maelstrom to summon an orbiting soul orb that deals heavy Arts damage to your units that collide into it.

enemy_2075_skgly2.name: Champion of Lost Agonies
enemy_2075_skgly2.desc: The strongest Goliath warrior. Though he never mentions his name, this champion is destined to become one of the Six Heroes. He has chosen to forsake his mortal wounds, giving his all to his trusted friend and king.
enemy_2075_skgly2.ability:
Attacks deal Necrosis
The next attack deals heavy Physical damage to the target. Recovers all SP if this skill kills the target or if the target retreats.

enemy_1403_gunsho_2.name: Heavy Rapid Crossbowman
enemy_1403_gunsho_2.desc: A heavy crossbowman who can fire three bolts at once. The Columbian investigation team was forced to admit that these 'homemade gadgets' were just as powerful as some of the latest cutting-edge weaponry.
enemy_1403_gunsho_2.ability: Launches a Straight Shot when positioned in a straight line with an allied unit: fires 3 bolts in the target's direction

enemy_1408_scotcn_2.name: Battlefield Surveying Mortarman
enemy_1408_scotcn_2.desc: A mortarman with a wealth of experience, who fires shells embedded with reconnaissance units. It is said that two military leaders once even fought to claim a senior mortarman.
enemy_1408_scotcn_2.ability:
Capable of extremely long ranged-attacks
Shells deal area damage to target and any allied units in the surrounding 8 tiles, and exposes targets for 5s
When Exposed, allied units cannot use barricades to keep themselves out of enemy's sight, and take more damage

enemy_1212_mtrfol_2.name: Pathfinder Legionarius Prefect
enemy_1212_mtrfol_2.desc: A powerful warrior who has flocked to the Guide, inspired by his philosophy. Fights to bring the common goal of the Pathfinders to fruition.

enemy_1215_ptrarc_2.name: Pathfinder Veles Prefect
enemy_1215_ptrarc_2.desc: An elite warrior who specializes in dropping into battle from the skies. In addition to defeating their enemies, they will also destroy their supplies in the blink of an eye, blowing the battle wide open.
enemy_1215_ptrarc_2.ability:
Able to land directly onto the battlefield.
Prioritizes attacking 'Gelato Stops'. Deals additional Arts Damage to 'Gelato Stops'.

enemy_1183_mlasrt_2.name: Armorless Union Cleanup Squad Leader
enemy_1183_mlasrt_2.desc: A leader of the cleanup squad personally trained by Roy. They lead the squad in place of Roy, who refuses to work, and are very experienced. The squad has adopted some kind of bounty system and is often very thorough in their tasks. Their attacks inflict Corrosion.
enemy_1183_mlasrt_2.ability:
Every attack deals Corrosion
After several attacks, the next attack deals more damage

relics.name: Pitt's Assorted Fruits
relics.desc: With rich taste and affordable prices, Pitt's assorted fruit candies rival Mama John's in the sweets market. Muelsyse's favorite.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +3, all allied units gain +3% ATK, DEF, and HP

relics.name: Featured Canned Meat
relics.desc: For travelers on a long journey, the convenience of 'ready to eat' is often more important than taste.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +1, all allied units recover 3 HP per second

relics.name: Topographical Map
relics.desc: A hand-drawn map exuding antiquity. The labels and terrain are enlarged, and its scale seems to be off.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +1, all allied units gain +4% DEF

relics.name: Conch of Divine Whispers
relics.desc: Sound it, and gods you've never seen or even heard of will answer your call. Actually, it's just a hollow conch shell.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +1, all allied units gain +4% HP

relics.name: Night-Whispers in the Forest
relics.desc: A Convalescent Garden aromatherapy set for field operators, featuring fifteen herbal extracts in a hundred and seventy-two combinations. Said to evoke the soothing sense of rustling leaves and gentle chirping.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +2, all allied units gain +6% DEF

relics.name: Church Alms Meal Voucher
relics.desc: In the declining coastal towns of Iberia, certain churches will provide alms in the form of meals, which their believers can redeem using vouchers. As for how these vouchers are distributed, that is a secret.
relics.usage: Deployment Limit +2, all allied units gain +6% HP

relics.name: Pried Open Toolbox
relics.desc: The tool box of the troupe's prop master. From nails to dummy props, it has everything.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Banshee's Kiss
relics.desc: Outbloods show exceptional courage when in possession of this trinket, but to Logos, a rare male Banshee, it's just a common object.
relics.usage: Hope +6, immediately gain 6 Originium Ingots

relics.name: Universal Key
relics.desc: An old, silver key. It can open any door. As the keeper, the troupe's butler naturally needs a key that unlocks all the secrets here.
relics.usage: Hope +6

relics.name: Seaweed Salad
relics.desc: Once a beloved Iberian delicacy, now fading from memory due to the rampaging Seaborn.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +1, all allied ranged units gain +2% ATK

relics.name: Orange Storm
relics.desc: A Mama John's candy. The orange flavor, cheap and delicious, is a best-seller among children.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +1, all allied melee units gain +2% ATK

relics.name: Coffee Plains Coffee Candy
relics.desc: Dossoles' famous coffee-flavored candy product. Has real caffeine added, to keep you wide awake from clock-in to overtime.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +2, all allied ranged units gain +3% ATK

relics.name: Screaming Cherry
relics.desc: A Mama John's candy. Though the niche vinegar-pickled cherry flavor can't compete with the best-sellers in popularity, it has garnered a small group of loyal supporters. Rumor has it that one of Mama John's directors loves this flavor.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +2, all allied melee units gain +3% ATK

relics.name: Antiquated Sheet Music
relics.desc: A tattered piece of sheet music. The notes can no longer be clearly read. No one knows for certain what story this piece was meant to tell.
relics.usage: Hope +3, all allied ranged units gain +4% ATK

relics.name: Letter of Termination Contract
relics.desc: You can break free from this troupe with just one signature.
relics.usage: Hope +4

relics.name: Blunt Claws - Training
relics.desc: A formidable set of equipment to provide strength, an indomitable heart to press forward bravely.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Vanguard Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Bend Spears - Training
relics.desc: Some people use swords because they don't know how to use crossbows or Arts. Others use swords because that's all they need.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Guard Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Iron Guard - Training
relics.desc: Scars symbolize glory, but who will remember the soldiers who were cut a bit too deeply?
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Defender Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Fatal Bolts - Training
relics.desc: 'Either you learn to control your fire power, or you learn to discern your target. Got it?'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Sniper Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Broken Wand - Training
relics.desc: 'After watching the 'performances' of certain casters, I decided to quietly put away the Leithanian textbook.'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Caster Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Stalwart Aid - Training
relics.desc: Their tactics are always diverse and colorful.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Supporter Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Healer's Path - Training
relics.desc: When the day comes that everyone is a doctor capable of curing the disease known as life, we will no longer need doctors.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Medic Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Rusted Blade - Training
relics.desc: When conducting live combat drills with Specialist Operators, please be prepared to be 'beaten up thoroughly before you are prepared.'
relics.usage: Immediately Promote a Specialist Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Game Room Admin Access Card
relics.desc: With this, you have become master over Rhodes Island's entertainment privileges. Please exercise caution when processing the various strange applications from the operators... and remember to lock the door every day.
relics.usage: Immediately Promote an Operator (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Miss.Christine Petting Ticket
relics.desc: You have the lady's permission!
relics.usage: Immediately Promote 2 Operators (Does not require Hope)

relics.name: Hot Water Kettle
relics.desc: Some people often use the hot water kettle in the Rhodes Island Office to cook instant noodles in the middle of the night. That's not a very healthy lifestyle...
relics.usage: +1 Max Life Point, +1 Hope

relics.name: Extra-Pungent Coffee Beans
relics.desc: Coffee beans fed to a rare, peculiar-smelling fowlbeast and processed from its excrement. The unique aroma from gastric acid fermentation triples the beans' price.
relics.usage: Squad Size Limit +1, Deployment Limit +1, +1 Objective Shield

relics.name: Victorian 'Scrap' Medal
relics.desc: A knight's medal that Siege threw together and awarded to Dagda on the spot. The materials used are pitiful, but it holds a lot of meaning to Dagda.
